title: oxytropis lunelliana
intro: Oxytropis lunelliana, also known as Lunell's locoweed, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It is a perennial herb that grows in the mountainous regions of North America, particularly in the western United States and Canada. Oxytropis lunelliana has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may provide relief from various health issues. Herbal preparations of Oxytropis lunelliana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a food source for livestock, although its consumption by humans is not well documented.
